progress_summary: Session pivoted from v1.4 implementation into v1.4 planning. Approved an 8-PR pack-registry architecture: replaces 5 hardcoded stacks with skills/stack-forge/packs/<id>/pack.yml + reference docs, drastically expanding to 11+ languages and 9 platforms (App Store included via PR 6 swift-ios). User explicitly pulled scope INTO the release rather than spreading across patches. Distribution stays bundle-everything; per-pack flags filter visibility, not download. Linear updated end-to-end: parent ADEV-327, PR tickets ADEV-328-337 (sans 333), patch tracker ADEV-332. Saved stack-forge.checkpoint.json as the single source of truth for the v1.4 plan; merged via PR #187 (squash, all 4 CI checks green). LHCI bot surfaced 4 pre-existing a11y failures unrelated to the doc-only PR; user authorized standing them up as a separate workstream. Cut a11y branch off main, filed ADEV-338 parent + ADEV-339/340/341/342 children, no code written yet on either track.

progress_table.label: Intake: 1.4 scope expansion (5 langs → 11+ langs + iOS)
Linear: ADEV-327 parent + ADEV-328-337 PR sub-issues
PR #187 merged (squash) — v1.4 plan checkpoint on main
Triage LHCI a11y failures from #187 build
Linear: ADEV-338 parent + 4 sub-issues (TOC, code, bands, links)
v1.4 PR 1 — schema, generator, auto-flag wiring (ADEV-328)

key_decisions: v1.4 theme: coverage-as-a-plugin-registry. skills/stack-forge/packs/<id>/pack.yml + reference docs replace hardcoded stack tables.
Apple/iOS ships in v1.4.0 (PR 6) — pressure-tests the kind: mobile dispatch branch. Android/Flutter/RN deferred to v1.5 once release-ops handles Play Store + EAS.

Distribution model: bundle-everything in opchain-skills.zip. Per-pack flags (skills.coverage.<pack-id>.enabled) filter what stack-forge OFFERS, not what's downloaded — keeps regulated/air-gapped installs intact.
5 skills version-bump 1.2.0 → 1.4.0 (stack-forge, api-dev, deploy-ops, app-architect, migration-ops). Other ~13 skills stay at 1.2.0.
8-PR sequencing: PRs 1-3 serial (architecture → backfill → dispatcher), PRs 4-7 parallelizable, PR 8 last (release).
ADEV-333 was created in error during scope shift; cancelled as duplicate of ADEV-332.

LHCI a11y failures on #187 (TOC touch size, code-block contrast, band labels, changelog links) are pre-existing — unrelated to the JSON-only diff. Standing up as ADEV-338 family on a separate branch off main, NOT BLOCKING v1.4.
user_preferences: Pulls scope INTO releases when it expands the value prop; comfortable with bigger releases when the PR sequence is clear.
Asks sharp clarifying questions about user-visible mechanics (downloads vs visibility, plugin pattern) before approving implementation.
Wants Linear kept current with the actual plan — re-aligns tickets when scope shifts.
Approves merges promptly once CI is green; deploys are always manual.
Skips non-actionable bot replies on PRs (no noise back to LHCI bot).

next_actions: Pick a track. v1.4 PR 1 (ADEV-328) is the highest-leverage starting point — it unblocks PRs 2-7. a11y cleanup (ADEV-338) is small and parallelizable; can land as a single PR in <1 session.

If picking v1.4: read stack-forge.checkpoint.json `next_actions[0]` — start with surveying src/lib/flags/registry.js, scripts/gen-flags.mjs, scripts/gen-skills-catalog.mjs, scripts/make-skills-zip.sh.

If picking a11y: confirm on `claude/a11y-lhci-cleanup`, then start with ADEV-339 (TOC touch-target — affects all skill pages, biggest visible win). Four commits (one per child ticket) so each fix is independently revertible.
Either branch needs `npm install` and `npm install --prefix site` before first build (lockfile drift detected this session was npm 10→11 metadata only — discarded with git restore).

linear_active_parents.title: opchain v1.4 — Drastically expand language & platform coverage
a11y cleanup — LHCI failing audits across /skills, /architecture, /changelog

skill_state.supersedes_prior_orchestrator_state: Prior session shipped #140/#143/#146/#148/#150/#151 and returned 'Session done — open a fresh session.' That work is now part of main; this session continues forward from there.
